name: Rogue
description: Rogues rely on cunning, stealth, and their foes' vulnerabilities to get the upper hand. They have a knack for finding solutions to any problem, making precise strikes over brute force. Some began as criminals, others as crime fighters.
weapon_proficiencies: Martial weapons with Finesse or Light property

Expertise.description: Gain Expertise in two skills of your choice. Sleight of Hand and Stealth are recommended if you have proficiency in them.

1.Sneak Attack: Once per turn, deal extra 1d6 damage with Finesse/Ranged weapon if you have Advantage or ally within 5 feet of target. The extra damage's type is the same as the weapon's type.

Thieves' Cant.description: You know Thieves' Cant and one other language of your choice.

1.Weapon Mastery: Use mastery properties of two weapons of your choice with which you have proficiency.

2.Cunning Action: Your quick thinking and agility allow you to take Dash, Disengage, or Hide as a Bonus Action on your turn.

3.Rogue Subclass: Choose your roguish archetype: Arcane Trickster, Assassin, Soulknife, or Thief.
3.Steady Aim: As a Bonus Action, give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this only if you haven't moved, and your Speed becomes 0 until the end of the turn.

4.Ability Score Improvement: Gain Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice.

5.Cunning Strike: When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can add one Cunning Strike effect by foregoing Sneak Attack dice. DC equals 8 + Dexterity modifier + Proficiency Bonus. Options: Poison (1d6, Con save or Poisoned 1 min, requires Poisoner's Kit), Trip (1d6, Dex save or Prone if Large or smaller), Withdraw (1d6, move half speed without provoking).
5.Uncanny Dodge: When an attacker you can see hits you with an attack roll, use your Reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

Expertise.description: Gain Expertise in two more skills of your choice.

7.Evasion: When subjected to a Dexterity saving throw for half damage, you take no damage on a success and half on a failure. You can't use this if you have the Incapacitated condition.
7.Reliable Talent: Whenever you make an ability check that uses one of your skill or tool proficiencies, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

8.Ability Score Improvement: Gain Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice.

10.Ability Score Improvement: Gain Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice.

11.Improved Cunning Strike: You can use up to two Cunning Strike effects when you deal Sneak Attack damage, paying the die cost for each effect.

12.Ability Score Improvement: Gain Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice.

14.Devious Strikes: New Cunning Strike options: Daze (2d6, Con save or limited to one action/move/bonus on next turn), Knock Out (6d6, Con save or Unconscious 1 min), Obscure (3d6, Dex save or Blinded until end of next turn).

Slippery Mind.description: Your mind is exceptionally difficult to control. You gain proficiency in Wisdom and Charisma saving throws.

16.Ability Score Improvement: Gain Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice.

17.Subclass Feature: Gain capstone feature from your archetype.

18.Elusive: No attack roll can have Advantage against you unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

19.Epic Boon: Gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice. Boon of the Night Spirit is recommended.

20.Stroke of Luck: If you fail a d20 Test, you can turn the roll into a 20. Once per Short or Long Rest.
